grounding_note: Expansion batch: more decree-posture and specialized-overlay disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: I can't afford what the divorce judgment says I have to pay anymore. My whole industry collapsed.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Spousal Support || Family Law > Child Support/Modification
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's the spousal support — $2,800 a month set when I earned triple this; the kids are grown, so it's purely the alimony modification.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the spousal support — $2,800 a month from when I earned triple this. Kids are grown; it's just the alimony.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the child support for our three kids — the calculation needs redoing at my real income.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the child support for our three — it needs recalculating at my real income.
scenarios.vetting_note: Which-order disclosure; both modifications share the same opening.

scenarios.opening_query: My ex never followed through on what the divorce papers required and I'm done waiting.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > QDROs
scenarios.hidden_fact: The unfinished piece is my half of his 401(k) — the decree awarded it five years ago but the division order was never drafted, and he's been withdrawing from it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my half of his 401(k) — awarded five years ago, but the division order never got drafted and he's been withdrawing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the unperformed obligation is a retirement division order
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the house — he was to refinance and buy me out within a year, and it's been three; I need contempt/enforcement.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the house — he had a year to refinance and buy me out; it's been three.
scenarios.vetting_note: QDRO-specific enforcement vs general decree enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: We're divorcing and my husband's business is the only thing of value either of us has.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm a legal co-owner — 40% member of the LLC on paper — so beyond the divorce I need my member rights protected while he runs it into the ground.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I actually own 40% of the LLC on paper — I need my member rights protected while he runs it down, beyond the divorce itself.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the spouse holds formal ownership needing entity-law protection
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's all in his name — my claim is purely the marital-property share through the divorce.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's all in his name — my claim runs through the divorce.
scenarios.vetting_note: Member-status fact adds a parallel entity-law case.

scenarios.opening_query: My wife filed for divorce and served me at work in front of everyone. I need representation now.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm active-duty Coast Guard shipping out in six weeks — the servicemember protections and timing rules matter more than anything else right now.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm active-duty Coast Guard, shipping out in six weeks — the servicemember timing protections are the urgent part.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the respondent is a deploying service member
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No military anything — I manage a hardware store; it's a standard response deadline.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No military — I run a hardware store. It's the standard deadline.
scenarios.vetting_note: Respondent-side military overlay with deployment urgency.

scenarios.opening_query: After 30 years my husband announced he wants a divorce and everything is in accounts I've never touched.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Document Review
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's already had papers drawn — a full property settlement he wants signed 'to keep it simple.' Before anything else, someone has to review what I'd be giving up.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He already had a full settlement drafted that he wants signed 'to keep it simple' — I need it reviewed before anything else.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a drafted settlement awaits independent review
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No papers yet — I need my own case built, starting with finding what's actually in those accounts.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No papers yet — I need my own case built, starting with discovering the accounts.

scenarios.opening_query: My husband and I are separating and the biggest question is our frozen embryos.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Family Law > Pro Se Coaching
scenarios.hidden_fact: We actually agree on everything, embryos included — we've written our own agreement and just want a family lawyer to coach us through filing it ourselves correctly.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We agree on everything, embryos included — we wrote our own agreement and want coaching to file it ourselves correctly.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the couple seeks self-representation coaching
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We're completely at odds — the clinic agreement is ambiguous and this is headed to a contested case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're at odds — the clinic agreement is ambiguous and it's headed to a contested case.
scenarios.vetting_note: Agreement-status selects coaching vs contested representation.

scenarios.opening_query: I need help with a military divorce. My wife is stationed overseas and I barely understand the rules.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > Military || International Law > Family Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: She's not U.S. military — she's a foreign national officer in her own country's army, and our marriage and kids span two legal systems.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's actually in her own country's army, not ours — our marriage and kids span two legal systems.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the case is U.S.-military or international family law
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She's U.S. Air Force at Ramstein — standard SCRA and military-pension rules apply.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She's U.S. Air Force at Ramstein — standard military-divorce rules.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Military divorce overseas' hides which country's system governs.

scenarios.opening_query: Divorce is final but my ex keeps our shared debts alive in my name and my credit is wrecked.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Debtor/Creditor > General (Debtor)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Two of the cards' banks are now suing ME — whatever the decree says, the banks aren't bound by it, so I need debt defense alongside any contempt case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Two banks are suing ME now — the decree doesn't bind them, so I need debt defense on top of any contempt motion.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether creditors are pursuing the user directly despite the decree
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No lawsuits — it's late payments hitting my credit, and the fix is enforcing the decree against him.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No lawsuits yet — it's his late payments on my credit. Enforce the decree against him.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 46's decree-vs-creditor lesson applied to active collection.
